title: Firmware Request : Sending GPS data in specified intervals or query GPS data

messages.author: BerndFfm
messages.text: I would like to enter an interval the adverts with gps data is send, e.g. every 5 minutes and 12 h if not moving. This should work without connected to an app. We will use this in search and rescue missions to locate vehicles, drones and mission forces in regions where there is no internet and mobile network. We also will use this in operations in other countries after earthquakes and natural disasters where there is no infrastructure. This feature can also be used at large concerts, on hiking tours and anywhere there is poor cell phone coverage. Also when working in the forest. It would be good to specify a scope like #sarmission for a very small area, so the MeshCore will not be bothered more than necessary. So this can be done with the following fields : [X] Live Tracking Intervall : 12 h Intervall when moving : 5 min Moving distance : 100 m Scope : sarmission Without scope this option should not work or scope is set to 'local'. Alternatively : Query GPS data from a device. The data is queried by the internal GPS receiver before sending, i.e. always the current position. Or you have a field how often gps is updated by the firmware e.g. 5 min. (edited)
messages.content_html: I would like to enter an interval the adverts with gps data is send, e.g. every 5 minutes and 12 h if not moving. This should work without connected to an app. We will use this in search and rescue missions to locate vehicles, drones and mission forces in regions where there is no internet and mobile network. We also will use this in operations in other countries after earthquakes and natural disasters where there is no infrastructure. This feature can also be used at large concerts, on hiking tours and anywhere there is poor cell phone coverage. Also when working in the forest. It would be good to specify a scope like #sarmission for a very small area, so the MeshCore will not be bothered more than necessary. So this can be done with the following fields : [X] Live Tracking Intervall : 12 h Intervall when moving : 5 min Moving distance : 100 m Scope : sarmission Without scope this option should not work or scope is set to 'local'. Alternatively : Query GPS data from a device. The data is queried by the internal GPS receiver before sending, i.e. always the current position. Or you have a field how often gps is updated by the firmware e.g. 5 min. (edited)

messages.author: Hugo
messages.content_html: Something like Meshcore TEAM's "Autonomous Mode"? https://github.com/tmacinc/MeshCore-TEAM#how-autonomous-mode-works
embeds_html:
GitHub - tmacinc/MeshCore-TEAM: Off-grid, telemetry based group tra...
Off-grid, telemetry based group tracking app utilizing MeshCore. Focused on Hunting and backcountry applications. - tmacinc/MeshCore-TEAM

messages.author: BerndFfm
messages.text: Yes, exactly like MeshCore TEAM or MeshCore SAR, but without a phone connected to the device. In a mission we give the T1000 Cards to people that don't have a special app on the phone. Our SAR dogs also will get a T1000 Card, they don't have a phone.
messages.content_html: Yes, exactly like MeshCore TEAM or MeshCore SAR, but without a phone connected to the device. In a mission we give the T1000 Cards to people that don't have a special app on the phone. Our SAR dogs also will get a T1000 Card, they don't have a phone.

messages.author: Hugo
messages.text: My understanding is that the Autonomous Mode for TEAM is exactly that: It requires their custom firmware, but specifically does work without a phone app connected. You: Load the custom firmware Use TEAM to set autonomous mode and set the GPS interval (time) or distance traveled threshold Periodic and distance-triggered GPS updates will then continue from the radio completely independently, even with a phone/app not connected From the linked section on Autonomous Mode specifically. (edited)
messages.content_html:
My understanding is that the Autonomous Mode for TEAM is exactly that: It requires their custom firmware, but specifically does work without a phone app connected. You:
  • Load the custom firmware
  • Use TEAM to set autonomous mode and set the GPS interval (time) or distance traveled threshold
  • Periodic and distance-triggered GPS updates will then continue from the radio completely independently, even with a phone/app not connected From the linked section on Autonomous Mode specifically.
 (edited)

messages.author: Christopher A. Wichura
messages.text: You can also configure the node to allow telemetry requests. Then, instead of it burping periodically, you just ask it from a registered contact what its location is. The telemetry includes the current GPS reading. This is supported by mainline firmware today. (edited)
messages.content_html: You can also configure the node to allow telemetry requests. Then, instead of it burping periodically, you just ask it from a registered contact what its location is. The telemetry includes the current GPS reading. This is supported by mainline firmware today. (edited)
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: BerndFfm
messages.text: Thank You Christopher. I will test it and it would be great if it meet our requirements. You will be a hero !
messages.content_html: Thank You Christopher. I will test it and it would be great if it meet our requirements. You will be a hero !
